papers.title: HeartMuLa: A Family of Open Sourced Music Foundation Models
papers.titleJa: HeartMuLa: オープンソースの音楽基盤モデルファミリー
papers.what: 音楽の理解と生成を多様なタスクやモダリティにわたって向上させるためのオープンソース音楽基盤モデルのファミリーを提案。
papers.novel: 学術規模のデータとGPUリソースを使用して、商業レベルの音楽生成システムを再現可能にした点が革新的。
papers.method: 音声-テキストアライメントモデル（HeartCLAP）、歌詞認識モデル（HeartTranscriptor）、高忠実度音楽コーデックトークナイザー（HeartCodec）、およびLLMベースの楽曲生成モデル（HeartMuLa）を含む4つの主要コンポーネントを統合。
papers.validation: HeartMuLaの性能を7Bパラメータにスケールアップすることで、商業レベルのシステムに匹敵する性能を実現したことを示した。
papers.discussion: 学術規模のリソースで商業レベルのシステムを再現可能にしたが、さらなるスケーラビリティや多様な音楽ジャンルへの適応性が課題。

nextReads.label: MusicLM: Generating Music From Text (2023)
Jukebox: A Generative Model for Music (2020)
MuLan: A Joint Embedding of Music Audio and Natural Language (2022)
Riffusion: Music Generation with Stable Diffusion (2022)

papers.abstract: We present a family of open-source Music Foundation Models designed to advance large-scale music understanding and generation across diverse tasks and modalities. Our framework consists of four major components: (1) HeartCLAP, an audio-text alignment model; (2) HeartTranscriptor, a robust lyric recognition model optimized for real-world music scenarios; and (3) HeartCodec, a low-frame-rate (12.5 Hz) yet high-fidelity music codec tokenizer that captures long-range musical structure while preserving fine-grained acoustic details and enabling efficient autoregressive modeling; (4) HeartMuLa, an LLM-based song generation model capable of synthesizing high-fidelity music under rich, user-controllable conditions (e.g., textual style descriptions, lyrics, and reference audio). In addition, it provides two specialized modes: (i) fine-grained musical attribute control, which allows users to specify the style of different song sections (e.g., intro, verse, chorus) using natural language prompts; and (ii) short, engaging music generation, which is suitable as background music for short videos. Lastly, HeartMuLa improves significantly when scaled to 7B parameters. For the first time, we show that a Suno-level, commercial-grade system can be reproduced using academic-scale data and GPU resources. We expect these foundation models to serve as strong baselines for future research and to facilitate practical applications in multimodal content production.

papers.title: Unifying Speech Recognition, Synthesis and Conversion with Autoregressive Transformers
papers.titleJa: 音声認識、合成、変換を統一する自己回帰型トランスフォーマー
papers.what: 音声認識（ASR）、音声合成（TTS）、音声変換（VC）を統一的に処理できるオーディオ基盤モデルGPAを提案。
papers.novel: 単一の自己回帰モデルで複数の音声タスクを統一的に処理できる設計を実現し、効率性とスケーラビリティを向上させた点が新規性。
papers.method: 離散オーディオトークンスペースを共有し、タスク誘導型の自己回帰モデルを用いてマルチタスク学習を行うアーキテクチャ。
papers.validation: ASR、TTS、VCタスクにおいて、軽量な0.3Bパラメータのモデルでも競争力のある性能を示し、低遅延での実用的展開が可能であることを確認。
papers.discussion: 統一的なアーキテクチャの有効性を示したが、さらなる性能向上や特定タスクへの最適化が課題。

nextReads.label: SpeechT5: Unified-Modal Encoder-Decoder Pre-Training for Spoken Language Processing (2022)
Whisper: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision (2022)
VALL-E: Neural Codec Language Models are Zero-Shot Text to Speech Synthesizers (2023)
SpeechStew: Simply Mix All Available Speech Recognition Data to Train One Large Neural Network (2021)

papers.abstract: Traditional speech systems typically rely on separate, task-specific models for text-to-speech (TTS), automatic speech recognition (ASR), and voice conversion (VC), resulting in fragmented pipelines that limit scalability, efficiency, and cross-task generalization. In this paper, we present General-Purpose Audio (GPA), a unified audio foundation model that integrates multiple core speech tasks within a single large language model (LLM) architecture. GPA operates on a shared discrete audio token space and supports instruction-driven task induction, enabling a single autoregressive model to flexibly perform TTS, ASR, and VC without architectural modifications. This unified design combines a fully autoregressive formulation over discrete speech tokens, joint multi-task training across speech domains, and a scalable inference pipeline that achieves high concurrency and throughput. The resulting model family supports efficient multi-scale deployment, including a lightweight 0.3B-parameter variant optimized for edge and resource-constrained environments. Together, these design choices demonstrate that a unified autoregressive architecture can achieve competitive performance across diverse speech tasks while remaining viable for low-latency, practical deployment.

papers.title: MoST: Mixing Speech and Text with Modality-Aware Mixture of Experts
papers.titleJa: MoST: 音声とテキストを統合するモダリティ対応エキスパート混合モデル
papers.what: 音声とテキストをシームレスに統合する新しいマルチモーダル大規模言語モデルMoSTを提案。
papers.novel: モダリティに応じた専門家グループと共有専門家を組み合わせた新しいルーティングメカニズムを導入し、モダリティ固有の学習とクロスモーダル理解を向上。
papers.method: Modality-Aware Mixture of Experts (MAMoE)アーキテクチャを採用し、ASRやTTSデータセットでの後処理と音声-テキスト指示データセットでの微調整を行う効率的な変換パイプラインを構築。
papers.validation: ASR、TTS、音声言語モデリング、音声質問応答ベンチマークで既存モデルを上回る性能を示し、モダリティ固有のルーティングメカニズムの有効性を確認。
papers.discussion: オープンソースデータセットのみを用いて高性能を達成したが、さらなるスケーラビリティや他のモダリティへの適用可能性が課題。

nextReads.label: SpeechT5: Unified-Modal Encoder-Decoder Pre-Training for Spoken Language Processing (2022)
Whisper: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision (2022)
Flamingo: A Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning (2022)
Gato: A Generalist Agent (2022)

papers.abstract: We present MoST (Mixture of Speech and Text), a novel multimodal large language model that seamlessly integrates speech and text processing through our proposed Modality-Aware Mixture of Experts (MAMoE) architecture. While current multimodal models typically process diverse modality representations with identical parameters, disregarding their inherent representational differences, we introduce specialized routing pathways that direct tokens to modality-appropriate experts based on input type. MAMoE simultaneously enhances modality-specific learning and cross-modal understanding through two complementary components: modality-specific expert groups that capture domain-specific patterns and shared experts that facilitate information transfer between modalities. Building on this architecture, we develop an efficient transformation pipeline that adapts the pretrained MoE language model through strategic post-training on ASR and TTS datasets, followed by fine-tuning with a carefully curated speech-text instruction dataset. A key feature of this pipeline is that it relies exclusively on fully accessible, open-source datasets to achieve strong performance and data efficiency. Comprehensive evaluations across ASR, TTS, audio language modeling, and spoken question answering benchmarks show that MoST consistently outperforms existing models of comparable parameter counts. Our ablation studies confirm that the modality-specific routing mechanism and shared experts design significantly contribute to performance gains across all tested domains. To our knowledge, MoST represents the first fully open-source speech-text LLM built on a Mixture of Experts architecture. \footnote{We release MoST model, training code, inference code, and training data at https://github.com/NUS-HPC-AI-Lab/MoST

papers.title: Robust CAPTCHA Using Audio Illusions in the Era of Large Language Models: from Evaluation to Advances
papers.titleJa: 大規模言語モデル時代における音声錯覚を利用した堅牢なCAPTCHA: 評価から進展まで
papers.org: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China
papers.what: 音声CAPTCHAの堅牢性を評価し、音声錯覚を活用した新しいCAPTCHA手法IllusionAudioを提案。
papers.novel: 音声錯覚を利用して人間には容易だが自動化された攻撃には困難なCAPTCHAを設計し、既存の音声CAPTCHAの脆弱性を克服。
papers.method: AI-CAPTCHAフレームワークを構築し、LALMやASRベースの攻撃に対する評価ツールACEvalと、新しい音声CAPTCHA手法IllusionAudioを提案。
papers.validation: 7種類の既存音声CAPTCHAを評価し、IllusionAudioがすべての攻撃を防ぎつつ100%の人間通過率を達成することを実証。
papers.discussion: 音声錯覚を活用した新手法の有効性を示したが、より多様な攻撃シナリオへの対応が今後の課題。

nextReads.label: Audio CAPTCHA: Existing Solutions and Future Directions (2021)
ReCAPTCHA: Human-Based Character Recognition via Web Security Measures (2008)
Adversarial Examples for Automatic Speech Recognition: Attacks and Countermeasures (2019)
Audio CAPTCHA Security in the Era of AI (2020)

papers.abstract: CAPTCHAs are widely used by websites to block bots and spam by presenting challenges that are easy for humans but difficult for automated programs to solve. To improve accessibility, audio CAPTCHAs are designed to complement visual ones. However, the robustness of audio CAPTCHAs against advanced Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) and Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models remains unclear.   In this paper, we introduce AI-CAPTCHA, a unified framework that offers (i) an evaluation framework, ACEval, which includes advanced LALM- and ASR-based solvers, and (ii) a novel audio CAPTCHA approach, IllusionAudio, leveraging audio illusions. Through extensive evaluations of seven widely deployed audio CAPTCHAs, we show that most existing methods can be solved with high success rates by advanced LALMs and ASR models, exposing critical security weaknesses.   To address these vulnerabilities, we design a new audio CAPTCHA approach, IllusionAudio, which exploits perceptual illusion cues rooted in human auditory mechanisms. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method defeats all tested LALM- and ASR-based attacks while achieving a 100% human pass rate, significantly outperforming existing audio CAPTCHA methods.

papers.title: FOCAL: A Novel Benchmarking Technique for Multi-modal Agents
papers.titleJa: FOCAL: マルチモーダルエージェントのための新しいベンチマーク手法
papers.what: 音声とテキストをサポートするマルチモーダルエージェントのエンドツーエンドの推論能力やエラー伝播を評価するための新しいベンチマークフレームワークFOCALを提案。
papers.novel: エージェントの推論能力とエラー分析を評価するための新しい指標（ReasoningスコアとSemanticスコア）を導入し、音声モードでの会話の有効性を測定可能にした点が新規性。
papers.method: FOCALは、エージェントのエンドツーエンド推論、コンポーネントごとのエラー伝播、およびエラー分析を自動化または人間支援によるテストで評価するフレームワーク。
papers.validation: 提案したReasoningスコアとSemanticスコアを用いて、マルチモーダルエージェントの音声モードでの会話能力を評価する実験を実施。
papers.discussion: エラー伝播の詳細な分析が可能だが、実際の産業応用におけるさらなる検証が必要。

nextReads.label: Towards Unified Multimodal Conversational Agents (2022)
Evaluating Conversational Agents with Multi-modal Inputs (2021)
Error Propagation in Cascading Pipelines for NLP (2020)
Advances in Audio-Language Models (2023)

papers.abstract: With the recent advancements in reasoning capabilities, tool calling using MCP servers and Audio Language Models (ALMs), development and integration of multi-modal agents (with voice and text support) has come to the industry forefront. Cascading pipelines for voice agents still play a central role in the industry owing to their superior reasoning capabilities facilitated by LLMs. Although, cascading pipelines often present error propagation through the pipeline. We propose a framework, FOCAL to benchmark end-to-end reasoning, component-wise error propagation and error analysis for automated as well as human-assisted testing of multi-modal agents (voice to voice + text input). We also share two novel metrics viz. Reasoning and Semantic scores to evaluate efficacy of the agent in having meaningful conversations in voice mode.
papers.comment: We present a framework for evaluation of Multi-modal Agents consisting of Voice-to-voice model components viz. Text to Speech (TTS), Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Speech-to-text (STT)

papers.title: SEE: Signal Embedding Energy for Quantifying Noise Interference in Large Audio Language Models
papers.titleJa: SEE: 大規模音声言語モデルにおけるノイズ干渉を定量化するための信号埋め込みエネルギー
papers.what: 大規模音声言語モデル（LALM）のノイズ干渉に対するロバスト性を定量的に評価する新しい手法であるSignal Embedding Energy（SEE）を提案。
papers.novel: SEEは、モデルの内部表現から構造化された活性化部分空間に基づき、ノイズの影響をより正確に捉える新しい視点を提供し、従来の直感的なアプローチを超える点が新規性。
papers.method: SEEは、LALMの内部表現を解析し、ノイズ強度がモデルの性能に与える影響を定量化する指標を提供。これに基づき、ノイズ軽減のための新しい戦略を提案。
papers.validation: SEEがLALMの性能と0.98の高い相関を持つことを実験で確認し、従来の音声ノイズ除去手法よりも優れた結果を示した。
papers.discussion: 従来の音声中心のノイズ除去手法がLALMに適合しない場合があることを指摘し、LALM特有のノイズ感受性に基づく新たなアプローチの必要性を提言。

nextReads.label: Robustness of Audio Language Models (2022)
Noise Robustness in Speech Recognition Systems (2021)
Understanding Noise Sensitivity in Neural Networks (2020)
Advances in Large Audio Language Models (2023)

papers.abstract: Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) have been widely applied in real-time scenarios, such as in-car assistants and online meeting comprehension. In practice, audio inputs are often corrupted by device and environmental noise, leading to performance degradation. However, existing LALM studies on noise lack quantitative analysis and rely mainly on intuition and empirical observation, thus failing to understand practical robustness. To address this issue, we introduce Signal Embedding Energy (SEE), a method for quantifying the impact of noise intensity on LALM inputs, enabling the differentiation of LALM robustness in real-world deployments. SEE introduces a perspective based on structured activation subspaces derived from the model's internal representations, which more accurately captures its perception of noise than raw audio features. Across experiments, SEE exhibits a strong correlation with LALM performance, achieving a correlation of 0.98. Surprisingly, traditional audio denoising methods are only marginally effective for LALMs, and, in some cases, even increase SEE and impair performance. This suggests a mismatch between speech-centric denoising objectives and the noise sensitivity of modern LALMs. Therefore, we propose a mitigation strategy derived from SEE to denoise LALM inputs, outperforming existing denoising methods. This paper introduces a novel metric for noise quantification in LALMs, providing guidance for robustness improvements in real-world deployments.

papers.title: Quantitative Analysis of Proxy Tasks for Anomalous Sound Detection
papers.titleJa: 異常音検知のための代理タスクの定量的分析
papers.org: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
papers.what: 異常音検知（ASD）の性能向上に向けて、自己教師付き代理タスクの有効性を定量的に分析。
papers.novel: 代理タスクの性能とASD性能の関係を体系的に分析し、特定のタスクが有効性を持つ理由を明らかにした点が新規性。
papers.method: 自己教師付き学習の5つの設定（オートエンコーダ、分類、音源分離、コントラスト学習、事前学習モデル）を用いて、線形分離性や分布のコンパクト性を評価。
papers.validation: 代理タスクの性能とASD性能の相関を実験的に検証し、特に音源分離タスクが最も高い相関を持つことを確認。
papers.discussion: 代理タスクの難易度や目標との整合性が重要であることを示したが、他のタスクの改良や新しいタスクの提案が必要。

nextReads.label: Anomalous Sound Detection Based on Autoencoder with Regularization (2020)
Self-supervised Learning for Audio Anomaly Detection (2021)
Anomaly Detection with Machine Learning: A Survey (2020)
Contrastive Learning for Anomaly Detection in Time Series Data (2022)

papers.abstract: Anomalous sound detection (ASD) typically involves self-supervised proxy tasks to learn feature representations from normal sound data, owing to the scarcity of anomalous samples. In ASD research, proxy tasks such as AutoEncoders operate under the explicit assumption that models trained on normal data will increase the reconstruction errors related to anomalies. A natural extension suggests that improved proxy task performance should improve ASD capability; however, this relationship has received little systematic attention. This study addresses this research gap by quantitatively analyzing the relationship between proxy task metrics and ASD performance across five configurations, namely, AutoEncoders, classification, source separation, contrastive learning, and pre-trained models. We evaluate the learned representations using linear probe (linear separability) and Mahalanobis distance (distributional compactness). Our experiments reveal that strong proxy performance does not necessarily improve anomalous sound detection performance. Specifically, classification tasks experience performance saturation owing to insufficient task difficulty, whereas contrastive learning fails to learn meaningful features owing to limited data diversity. Notably, source separation is the only task demonstrating a strong positive correlation, such that improved separation consistently improves anomaly detection. Based on these findings, we highlight the critical importance of task difficulty and objective alignment. Finally, we propose a three-stage alignment verification protocol to guide the design of highly effective proxy tasks for ASD systems.
papers.comment: 13 pages, 5 figures, Submitted to IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing

trend: ① 音声とテキストを統合するマルチモーダル技術が進展しており、MoSTやGPAが音声認識、合成、変換を統一的に処理する新たな基盤モデルとして注目されている。
② 音楽や音声の生成・理解を支援する基盤モデル（HeartMuLa）や異常音検知の性能向上を目指す代理タスク分析が進み、応用範囲が拡大している。
③ 音声CAPTCHA（IllusionAudio）やノイズ干渉評価（SEE）など、音声AIの堅牢性や評価手法に関する研究が進展している。
